title: Sadopaideia
description: Sadopaideia is a erotic novel by Ashantee of Edinburgh. Ruled indecent in 1969.
descriptionParagraphs: Sadopaideia is a erotic novel by Ashantee of Edinburgh. Ruled indecent in 1969.

Its interest lies partly in the way literary or informational writing gets collapsed into a public-morality problem. As a erotic novel, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in New Zealand. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Ruled indecent in 1969. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: Sadopaideia is a erotic novel by Ashantee of Edinburgh. Ruled indecent in 1969.
banningParagraphs: Sadopaideia entered censorship debates as a erotic novel associated with morality, print scandal, and sexuality. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around obscenity and public morality.
The earliest event currently captured here is 1969 in New Zealand, where Indecent Publications Tribunal classified, prohibited, or restricted. Ruled indecent in 1969. Ruled indecent in 1969.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: Literature Suppressed on Sexual Grounds
counterReadings.author: Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: Surveys the legal and moral language used to suppress books as obscene.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.
